context: External stabilization session for Pi runtime pressure. TASK-BUD-1089 is now completed as a report-only development velocity pressure primitive. The primitive detects session/runtime pressure, dirty scope, board size, handoff age, useful commit age, process age, memory/disk, host VHDX pressure, stale active agent-run registry entries, and ceremony/tool-call pressure. Current machine pressure still blocks long Pi runs: C: has about 3GB free, with Docker/WSL VHDX artifacts accounting for about 214GB.

Re-run npm run ops:disk:cleanup:dry and npm run pi:dev:pressure after cleanup.
Run npm run release:readiness:v0.8.0 after disk pressure clears and choose the next P0 from the ready-to-start list.
Prefer small validated slices until machine-disk-pressure is no longer blocking.
